. . . . LOADED {{Class 0|safety=Unsafe|security=Chemical Hazard|entity=Atomic Disruption|class=ε|hexagon=ε|color-h=231}} '''Level 127''' is the '''128th''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. =
Description:
= {{Blurimage|caption=05/17/2022, A computer console I found in the middlea consistently cool and comfortable 16°C, and it is unknown whether Level 127 has a day or night cycle due to the constant dull glow of light of nowhere. Has power and a stack of hard drives.|file=ZomboDroid 22032026162944.jpg}} '''Level 127''' is a mostly barren, desolate wasteland, covered with a thick, mildly toxic fog of sulfur dioxide. The entirety of Level 127 is organized into a grid-like pattern, with each square measuring 5 square kilometers. The borders of the grids can be noted by the stark lack of vegetation and features, forming straight lines and intersections where the squares meet. Note that all of the vegetation on '''Level 127''' is inedible, dead, and dried up. Judging by the vegetation's appearance, they appear to have been dead for a long time. The temperature is at a consistent cool and comfortable 16°C, and it is unknown whether or not Level 127 has a day or night cycle due to the constant dull glow of light coming from the fog. {{Blurimage|caption=05/17/2022, Dead vegetation... Hopefully, I will get the same fate soon...|file=Level-127-GOODPT2.png}}As a wanderer traverses the expanses of these badlands, often a ruin of some random building or structure can be found in one of the squares. Wanderers call these structures "monuments" because of the sense of remembrance surrounding most of them. Some quadrants, seemingly sparse and randomly distributed across the level, will be filled with acidic water. The pH of the water is about 2.2 to 2.5 and is assumed to be diluted sulfuric acid due to the smell of 'rotten eggs' in its vicinity. This is theorized to be the main source of the acidity and toxicity of the fog present on the level. Other quadrants, which are even rarer to find, appear to be corrupted and glitchy. These areas are highly unstable and prone to no-clipping. Wanderers that entered these grid spaces have noted the feeling of static throughout their bodies. It is possible to find a square which is entirely missing. In its place is simply a 5x5 kilometer window into [[The Void]]. These are the rarest anomalies found on Level 127. The sight is said to be extremely anxiety-inducing, as even the air above these squares appears to be entirely empty, lacking in light and the level's notorious fog. Wanderers that have ventured too close to the edge of these spaces have noted to have a difficult time breathing, as though even the oxygen in that space was somehow consumed by some sort of vacuum. It is presumed that wanderers that fall into these spaces are lost to [[The Void]] forever. {{Blurimage|caption=05/29/2022, Found a place to search, not much so far, seems to have been untouched but the power is still working...|file=Level127127.webp}} ====Terminals:==== In the exact middle of each grid, there are computer terminals, presumably x86 or 32-bit. The computers have old Intel Xeon processors with no dedicated internal graphics card. Each terminal has a hot-swappable hard drive bay with 5-10 SATA ports. Each hard drive found on Level 127 contains one or two files. The files are usually 'exe' files labeled with levels designations such as "Level_11.exe". Uncommonly one might find a file containing a text document with the executable, which is similarly labeled, opening the level file documentation. {{Blurimage|caption=05/29/2022, Another one of these terminals... They seem important.|file=16zgi61dlbd81.jpg}} When the 'exe' file is executed, the quadrat that the wanderer is standing in will begin to shift. It takes approximately 5-7 minutes to fully load an 'exe' file from a terminal hard drive. The area around the wanderer forms a mimic of the corresponding level the 'exe' file was named after. If, for example, Level_000.exe was loaded, the wanderer and the terminal would reside at the exact center of a replica of Level 0. There will be old, damp carpet, yellow monotone wallpaper, and bright, loud, fluorescent lights above. No entities or wanderers will generate inside of this replica, although it is not considered safe from the threats normally associated with any given level. These level replicas will keep their anomalous properties and states, including any natural disasters or any ill effect one may experience when traversing said level normally. When one gets a corrupted har. . . =ERROR 404 FILE NOT FOUND= Redirecting... . . . . . . . File cannot be retrieved. Review .txt file? (Y/N)

. . . . . C:\Users\null>cd \ C:\>dir /s /b /a-d *Level_* . . . C:\Users\null\Documents\newhome.txt . . >type C:\Users\null\Documents\newhome.txt . . . God... I never thought I'd end up here... Me, my home, my friends... all of them are here now... I'm banished to this hell, forced to look at my home decay over time. I will stay with my home, attempting to fix it. Even though Redston and I weren't on the best of terms... He is still my friend, and I hope his home is doing alright... If anyone sees this file, you need to find a way out of here. I've seen people pop out of existence for not being considered ''Important'' enough. Make sure to stick with people here, wandering alone is your wor. . . =ERROR 0x80070570 FILE CORRUPTED= . . . . . . Rebuilding Datafile... . . . . . . File rebuilt. Do you wish to continue? (Y/N)

. . . . C:\Users\null\Downloads\Level_127.txt LOADED ===Colonies And Outposts=== ====Outpost Epsilon-27==== *Houses around 9-14 members. *5 members at the outpost 24/7. *Run by the person they call '''The Owner''' *Outpost located at a bowling alley; a lot less run down than the other buildings. *Often can be seen handing out simple cloth masks to wanderers who stumble by. ===Entrances And Exits=== ====Entrances==== *Running any of the files found on a hard drive from this level, in any other level, will cause the wanderer to end up here. *In [[Level_66|Level 66]], enter a door that looks like this level. *If the wanderer passes out due to the air in [[Level_38|Level 38]], there is a high chance the wanderer ends up here. *If the wanderer tries to escape the radiation hazards in [[Level 289]] using the fume hoods, commonly, the wanderer will accidentally noclip through the table after the window is closed and end up here. ====Exits==== *Attempting to use the known exits in the levels that are rendered via the terminals in this level will cause the wanderer to end up in [[Level_404|Level 404]]. *Passing out due to the Sulfur Dioxide has been proven fatal, but in slim chances the wanderer has ended up in [[Level_105|Level 105]]. *No-clipping in corrupt squares can lead to [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Level_List any level], but each individual corrupt square will permanently corelate to said specific level. If that level ceases to exist, wanderers will simply fall into [[The Broken]]. *Jumping into a voided quadrat will presumably send a wanderer to [[The Void]]. Open Credits? (Y/N)
>Y[[File:Flickering cursor.gif|left|12x12px]]
